studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary and secondary outcome measures are collected at baseline and post-treatment (3 months after randomisation). Uncontrolled follow-up is collected 6 and 12 months after randomisation.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group and a waiting control group (Care-as-Usual).
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group receives direct access to the 7-unit online programme 'HelloBetter ratiopharm chronic pain' based on Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT). The control group is a waiting control group receiving Care-as-Usual (CAU) with unrestricted access to routine care, and is offered free access to the online programme after the 3-month data collection.
studies.collected_data: West Haven-Yale Multidimensional Pain Inventory (MPI) interference scale, Patient Health Questionnaire 9 Item Version (PHQ-9), Assessment of Quality of Life (AQOL-8D), Brief Pain Inventory (BPI), Pain Disability Index (PDI), Generalised Anxiety Disorder Scale-7 (GAD-7), Pain processing questionnaire (FESV), Chronic Pain Acceptance Questionnaire (CPAQ), Acceptance and Action Questionnaire II (FAH-II) / Psychological Inflexibility in Pain Scale (PIPS), Pain Self-Efficacy Questionnaire (PSEQ), Patient satisfaction questionnaire (CSQ-8 / ZUF-8), Negative effects questionnaire (NEQ), Subjective global assessment of change (PGIC).

studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The study includes three visits per patient: baseline (V1), telephone visit after four weeks (V2), and a visit at month three (V3).
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Group 1 Intervention: HelloBetter ACT-app; Group 2 Control: standard therapy without the app.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Group 1 receives the HelloBetter app, an interactive online behavioral therapy program (seven weekly units, 45-60 minutes each) in addition to standard therapy. Group 2 receives standard therapy without the app.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: change in the pain interference scale of the West Haven-Yale Multidimensional Pain Inventory (MPI-D) at month 3. Secondary outcomes/collected data: NRS global pain, back pain (BASDAI question 2), nocturnal back pain, inflammatory back pain (BASDAI questions 5 and 6), PASS status, ASDAS, BASDAI, BASFI, BASMI, HADS, mSQUASH, ASAS HI, FIQ, SF36, patient and physician global assessment, PAM12, patient global impression of change, painDetect questionnaire. Intervention group also completes MARS, system usability questionnaire, and questions on app benefit and recommendation.

studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Assessments take place before randomisation (T0), 9 weeks after randomisation (T1, post-intervention), and 6 months after randomisation (T2, follow-up).
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention 1: GET.ON chronic pain guided; Intervention 2: GET.ON chronic pain unguided; Control: Waitlist control group
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1 (Guided): 7 weekly lessons of GET.ON chronic pain with feedback emails from an eCoach after each lesson. Arm 2 (Unguided): 7 weekly lessons of GET.ON chronic pain without feedback emails (autonomous). Both active arms include information, interactive exercises, audio/video files, and an optional SMS-coach for motivational messages. Arm 3: Waitlist control group.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: Pain interference (MPI Interference-Scale). Secondary Outcomes: Demographic variables, prior pain treatments, type, chronicity, comorbidities, Brief Pain Inventory (BPI), Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9), Generalised Anxiety Disorder Screener (GAD-7), Pain intensity (11-point NRS), Quality of life (SF-12 and EQ-5D), Acceptance and Action Questionnaire-II (AAQ-II / FAH-II), Chronic Pain Acceptance Questionnaire (CPAQ-D), Trimbos/iMTA questionnaire for costs associated with psychiatric illness (TiC-P), Patient Global Impression of Change scale (PGIC), intervention adherence (attrition rate), and Client Satisfaction Questionnaire (CSQ-8 / ZUF-8).

audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title:
Possibility of the health app reducing the impact of pain on
patients with axial spondyloarthritis in everyday life. (ESTABLISH
study)
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title:
Effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of a guided and unguided internet-based acceptance and commitment therapy for chronic pain: 
a three-armed randomised controlled trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results
